title: Transmitting application data for on-device demos
abstract: A system and method to profile an application use and identify data used for application execution, map the identified data for application execution to a virtual memory associated with application execution, including execution beginning at specific times, states or stages of the application, and transmit the virtual memory to an end user wishing to demonstrate the application on an end user device. The end user device can emulate the application from any desired application start time, state or stage using data at the end user device identified by the virtual memory.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for providing an application demonstration, comprising: profiling an application execution at a profiling user device and capturing a snapshot of a profiling user device memory state while executing the application; identifying data used for executing at least a portion of the application; mapping the identified data for executing at least the portion of the application to a virtual memory; and transmitting the virtual memory to an end user device for application demonstration emulation. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising identifying the data to execute the application from the snapshot. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising mapping the identified data to a virtual memory associated with application execution. 4. The method of any of claims 2 to 3, further comprising receiving an application demonstration request from the end user device and in response, providing the virtual memory associated with the requested application demonstration to the end user device. 5. The method of any of claims 1 to 4, further comprising: dividing the virtual memory; and sequentially communicating the divided virtual memory to the end user device. 6. A method for providing an application demonstration, comprising: emulating an application demonstration starting at one of a desired application start time, desired application start state and desired application start stage, using input/output (10) data of a memory at an end user device identified by a virtual memory from an application provider; and providing an alert when the application emulation is no longer supported by the 10 data identified by the virtual memory. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising: sending an application demonstration request to the application provider, wherein the application demonstration request specifies one of a desired application start time, desired application start state and desired application start stage and in response, receiving the virtual memory from the application provider. 8. A system for providing an application demonstration, comprising: a profiler, configured to profile an application execution and identify data used for application execution, wherein the data used for application execution is sufficient to provide emulation of at least a portion of an application on an end user device; a virtual mapper, configured to map the identified data for application execution to a virtual memory; and a communication element, configured to transmit the virtual memory to an end user device for application demonstration emulation. 9. The system of claim 8, further comprising the end user device, configured to emulate the application using data at the end user device identified by the virtual memory. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the end user device comprises an on-device application emulator, configured to emulate the application using the data identified by the virtual memory at the end user device. 11. The system of claim 9 or 10, wherein the end user device further comprises an exception handler, configured to provide an alert when the application demonstration emulation is no longer supported by the IO data identified by the virtual memory. 12. The system of any of claims 8-11, wherein the profiler is further configured to profile the application execution at a profiling user device and to capture a snapshot of a profiling user device memory state while executing the application. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the profiler is further configured to identify the data to execute the application from the snapshot of the profiling user device memory state while executing the application. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the virtual mapper is further configured to map the identified data to a virtual memory associated with application execution. 15. The system of any of claims 8-14, wherein the communication element is further configured to receive an application demonstration request from the end user device and in response, provide the virtual memory associated with the requested application demonstration to the end user device. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the application demonstration request from the end user device specifies one of a desired application start time, desired application start state and desired application start stage. 17. The system of any of claims 8-16, wherein: the virtual mapper is further configured to divide the virtual memory; and the communication element is further configured to sequentially communicate the divided virtual memory to the end user device. 18. The system of any of claims 8-17, further comprising: an additional profiler, configured to profile an application execution and identify data required for application additional features execution; a virtual mapper, configured to map the identified data for application additional features execution to a virtual memory; and a communication element, configured to transmit the virtual memory to an end user device for application additional features demonstration emulation. 19. An end user device for an application demonstration, comprising: 

 an on-device application emulator, configured to emulate an application demonstration starting at one of a desired application start time, desired application start state and desired application start stage, using input/output (10) data of a memory at the end user device identified by a virtual memory from an application provider; and an exception handler, configured to provide an alert when the application emulation is no longer supported by the 10 data identified by the virtual memory. 20. The device of claim 19, further comprising: a communication element, configured to send an application demonstration request to the application provider, wherein the application demonstration request specifies one of a desired application start time, desired application start state and desired application start stage and in response, the communication element is configured to receive the virtual memory from the application provider.
